---
title: "Installation"
toc: true
---

You can install `repos` as a system package (Ubuntu/Debian, macOS, Windows), from
source, or as a language-specific wrapper (R or Python).

## Ubuntu / Debian

### Option 1: Install from APT Repository (Recommended)

Install and keep `repos` up to date directly via `apt`:

```bash
# Add repository signing key
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MiguelRodo/apt-miguelrodo/main/KEY.gpg \
   | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/miguelrodo-repos.gpg

# Add apt source
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/miguelrodo-repos.gpg] https://raw.githubusercontent.com/MiguelRodo/apt-miguelrodo/main/ ./" \
   | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/miguelrodo-repos.list >/dev/null

# Install repos
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install -y repos
```

To uninstall:

```bash
sudo apt-get remove repos
```

### Option 2: Local Installation (No sudo required)

Install to your user directory (`~/.local/bin`):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
cd repos
bash install-local.sh
```

If `~/.local/bin` is not on your PATH, add this to `~/.bashrc` or `~/.profile`:

```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
```

Then reload:

```bash
source ~/.bashrc
```

To uninstall:

```bash
bash uninstall-local.sh
```

### Option 3: System-wide from Release `.deb` (requires sudo)

Download and install the latest `.deb` from the
[Releases page](https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos/releases):

```bash
VERSION_REPOS=1.2.6
wget https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos/releases/download/v${VERSION_REPOS}/repos_${VERSION_REPOS}_all.deb
sudo dpkg -i repos_${VERSION_REPOS}_all.deb
rm repos_${VERSION_REPOS}_all.deb

# Fix any dependency issues
sudo apt-get install -f
```

To uninstall:

```bash
sudo dpkg -r repos
```

### Dependencies

All Ubuntu/Debian methods require `bash`, `git`, `curl`, and `jq`.  These are
typically pre-installed.  If not:

```bash
sudo apt-get install bash git curl jq
```

---

## macOS (Homebrew)

```bash
brew tap MiguelRodo/repos
brew install repos
```

The formula automatically handles the `jq` dependency. Git is typically
pre-installed on macOS.

---

## Windows

### Scoop (recommended)

```powershell
scoop bucket add repos https://github.com/MiguelRodo/scoop-bucket
scoop install repos
```

`git` and `jq` are installed automatically by Scoop. You will also need
[Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win) for Bash support.

### Manual

1. Clone the repository:
   ```powershell
   git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
   cd repos
   ```
2. Run the installer:
   ```powershell
   .\install.ps1
   ```
3. Restart your PowerShell session.

**Dependencies:**

- [Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win) — provides `bash`, `git`, and `curl`
- [jq](https://jqlang.github.io/jq/download/) — JSON processor

---

## From Source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
cd repos

# Local install (no sudo)
bash install-local.sh

# Or system-wide on Ubuntu/Debian
sudo dpkg-buildpackage -us -uc -b
sudo dpkg -i ../repos_*.deb
```

---

## Language Wrappers

### R Package

```r
install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("MiguelRodo/repos")
```

Requires `bash`, `git`, `curl`, and `jq` on your system.

### Python Package

```bash
pip install git+https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
```

Or from a local clone:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/MiguelRodo/repos.git
cd repos
pip install .
```

Requires `bash`, `git`, `curl`, and `jq` on your system.  On Windows, use
[Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win) or WSL.

---

## Verify Installation

```bash
repos --help
```
